calendar.matters:
ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Fees, Final Distribution
Off Calendar Jul-12-2023 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Richard Schlinder, Esq. (appearing by video conference) and Roger Rubin, Esq., present in the Courtroom, both for Brian McNamara, Executor.

Court and counsel review the error in the verification. Mr. Schlinder advises that once the Executor returns from vacation - on or after July 1, 2023 - he will execute a corrected verification and file the same with the Court.

Court and counsel discuss the want of detail for certain large expenses. Mr. Rubin advises that the AT&T bill as well as the PG&E bill reflect the unpaid balances that arose after the decedent's death as both the estate and the decedent's minor daughter were not in a position to pay bills until the estate acquired an EIN - inordinately delayed by the IRS - and could then take custody of various bank accounts. With respect to the automobile insurance, Mr. Rubin reports that the insurer was Progressive and that the amount was high due to the age of the minor daughter who was driving the decedent's car [the daughter was over the age of 16 years and is now 18 years old].

Regarding real property incumbrances, Mr. Rubin reports that there was a first and second mortgage on the property which the Executor brought current with his own funds and the second mortgage has now been paid in full.

Mr. Rubin notes that the Executor, as a close friend of the decedent and his family, advanced significant funds to provide for the minor daughter. He further advises that both the decedent and the daughter were estranged from the wife/mother who abandoned the family some time ago.

Mr. Rubin reports on the status of the decedent's painting business which has been wound down by a long-time employee of the business. There remain outstanding employment tax issues with the IRS. Mr. Rubin and Mr. Schlinder report on the status of the remaining bank accounts, including the Wells Fargo business account that has/had a balance of $6,139 which Mr. Rubin reports is now closed after payment of all business expenses.

After further discussion, the Court takes the matter under submission with the intention to grant the pending petition. However, the Court may only accept the accounting and not approve the same due to commingling of funds.

Judge: Hon. Stephen M. Murphy; Clerk: R. Michael Diles; Bailiff: Deputy Gomez. Reporter: Not Reported. (D204)

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Fees, Final Distribution
Off Calendar Apr-17-2023 Continued To Jun-27-23 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PROBATE ACCOUNTING STATUS PETITION
Off Calendar Jan-17-2023 Off Calendar

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate
Off Calendar Aug-30-2022 Off Calendar

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate
Off Calendar Jul-12-2022 Continued To Aug-30-22 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Richard Schindler and Roger Rubin, in court, for Brian Macnamara, Personal Representative.

The court points counsel to the outstanding Inventory and Appraisal ("I&A"). Mr. Schindler advises that the I&A has been delivered to the probate referee. The court reminds counsel that Letters were issued over a year ago and that the court needs them to file either a petition for final distribution or a petition for approval of report on status of administration, as well as the I&A, by the next calling of this matter.

After additional discussion, the matter is continued to Aug-30-2022.

Judge: Hon. Cecilia Castellanos. Examiner: Sandra Hilton. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Bastain. Not Reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate
Off Calendar May-24-2022 Continued To Jul-12-22 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Richard Schindler and Roger Rubin, in court, for Brian McNamara, Executor.

The court queries counsel as to the status of an Inventory and Appraisal ("I&A") and either a Petition for Final Distribution or a Petition for Approval of Report on Status of Administration. Mr. Rubin reports of delays in obtaining an EIN from the IRS, which the bank advised he required to access bank records. The court reminds counsel that the court issued Letters last year which should allow the Executor to access bank records. Mr. Rubin reports that the bank refused to work with the Executor without the EIN. Mr. Schindler advises of the information that counsel does have up to this point. The court reminds counsel that all of the information that they have provided should have been included in a Petition for Approval of Report on Status of Administration. The court confirms with counsel that the minor turns eighteen (18) in July of this year.

After additional discussion, the Status on Close of Estate is continued to Jul-12-2022 at 9:00 am in Dept. 204. Counsel is instructed to file a Inventory and Appraisal and either a Petition for Final Distribution or a Petition for Approval of Report on Status of Administration one week prior to the next calling of this matter or an appearance will be required.

Judge: Hon. Joseph Quinn. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Bastain. Not Reported.

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar May-19-2021 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel or Interested Person.

This matter was put on for hearing with no appearance required for the purpose of admitting the will. There being no objections and the matter being properly noticed, the Court granted the Petition for Probate of Will and for Letters Testamentary. The will dated December 15, 2020 is admitted to probate. Brian MacNamara is appointed as the Executor with full authority. Bond is not required. Frank Mahoney is appointed Probate Referee. Order for the same signed in open Court. Status on close of estate is set for May 24, 2022 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. If a petition for final distribution, or a petition for approval of report on status of administration is filed one week prior, the status hearing will go off calendar.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Tauscher. Not reported.
